title: The Knife of Stone
subtitle: On the eighth day, the Infant sheds His first blood and is named Jesus, Savior

stillness: Be still before the name that is above every name.
According to Bernard, it is honey in the mouth, a melody in the ear, a jubilee in the heart.
Hold it quietly within you: Jesus.
gospel: And after eight days were accomplished,
that the child should be circumcised,
according to the precept of the Law,
the name which was called by the Angel
before He was conceived in the womb.
behold: Consider the dignities of this name.
First, that this name was foreordained and consecrated from eternity.
It was named by the divine mouth;
desired by the Patriarchs and the ancient Fathers;
made known abroad by the Angels.
And this day a second great thing was done:
this day the Lord Jesus began to pour out His most sacred blood for us,
when His flesh was cut with a knife of stone.
The child Jesus wept this day, because of the pain He felt in His flesh;
for He took true flesh, and felt it both in soul and in body.
consider: Beginning from humility, which is the root and the guardian of the virtues, Jesus received the seal of circumcision.
He did not delay to pour out the price of His blood for you, that He might show Himself your truest Savior.
He who committed no sin began this day to bear the punishment for our sins.
Not in manhood only, but even in the age of infancy, He willed to shed His blood for us.
Early did He begin to suffer for us.
This is the name of which Anselm says: Jesus is a sweet name, a delightful name, a name that comforts the sinner, the name of blessed hope.
Therefore, O Jesus, be Jesus to me.
The name of Jesus has power: a power to wash away, as to the stain of sin; a power to sanctify, as to the guilt; a power to justify, as to the debt of punishment.
For there is no other name under heaven given to men, whereby we must be saved.
prayer: O merciful Jesus, who, born of the Virgin under the Law, willed to be circumcised:
circumcise, O merciful Jesus, the thoughts, the words, and the works of Your servant,
that I may carry through nothing contrary to Your will in thinking, in speaking, in working.
Behold, O Lord, before You are: my heart, my tongue, my senses and my members;
they strive indeed, but of themselves they cannot.
Do You that which they cannot, and perfect the desire of me, a sinner,
You who are accustomed to grant the just their desire. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Nomen quod, secundum Bernardum, est mel in ore, in aure melos, in corde jubilus.
latinJewel.english: A name which, according to Bernard, is honey in the mouth, a melody in the ear, a jubilee in the heart.
